Inversion Atlas

Browse local PCA windows across a chromosome to identify and characterize inversion candidates. Load a precomp JSON in the sidebar (Browse… on the left) to begin scrubbing.

tabs
1 diagnostic · main scrubber: sim_mat, robust |Z|, per-sample lines, K-means PCA, L3 contingency tables
2 candidate focus · deep-dive on a single promoted candidate
3 boundaries · refine each candidate's start/end into approximate boundary zones
4 catalogue · sortable, filterable table of inversions; export TSV / Markdown
5 karyotype · per-candidate sample-level regime breakdown
6 popstats · stack of population-genetic tracks aligned to the chromosome
7 ancestry · per-window ancestry view (K-cluster + delta12 confidence)
8 windows · per-window summary table (|Z|, λ₁/λ₂, SNP counts, L1/L2 membership)
9 confirmed · carousel of candidates marked confirmed on page 2
11 help · vocabulary, hotkeys, and the full feature roadmap

To produce a precomp JSON on the cluster:

Rscript export_precomp_to_json_v3.R --precomp <LGNN.rds> \
    --l1_envelopes ..._d17L1_envelopes.tsv \
    --l2_envelopes ..._d17L2_envelopes.tsv \
    --l2_boundaries ..._d17L2_boundaries.tsv \
    --out LG28.json

local PCA θπ — chromosome-wide diversity scanner
SCHEMA §22 · structural scaffold · panel renderers ship as R-side data lands
The idea
This page mirrors page 1's pipeline (per-window analysis → window×window similarity → MDS → cluster → candidate intervals) but reads per-sample nucleotide diversity (θπ) instead of dosage. Two scrubbers, two independent input signals, same shape of output.
A region that hits BOTH scrubbers' candidate lists is ~certainly real biology — that's the orthogonal validation. A region that hits ONLY this scrubber is something the genotype scrubber can't see (sweeps, balancing selection, Het-class subdivisions, mis-assemblies) — that's where new biology lives.
Required JSON layers (from cluster-side R-pipeline)
layer
R-side script
status
feeds panels
theta_pi_per_window
STEP_R39
⚪ not loaded
per-sample lines · θπ-PC inputs
theta_pi_local_pca
STEP_R40
⚪ not loaded
sim_mat · |Z| · K-means scatter
theta_pi_envelopes
STEP_R41
⚪ not loaded
L1/L2 envelopes · candidate intervals
Panel layout (mirrors page 1, top to bottom)
panel
page 1 equivalent
what it shows
#thCtrlBar
#ctrlBar
scrubber slider · play · view-mode (genome / L1 / L2 zoom)
#thSimPanel
#simPanel
θπ_sim_mat heatmap · L1 (blue) / L2 (green) overlays
#thZPanel
#zPanel
diversity-|Z| waveform · click to jump to window
#thLinesPanel
#linesPanel
per-sample θπ-PC1 lines · K=3 / K=6 coloring
#thPcaPanel
#pcaPanel
θπ-PC1 vs θπ-PC2 K-means scatter · current window
#thL3Panel
#l3Panel
diversity L3 contingency · per-band θπ subdivision
Scale — locked to win50000.step10000
ANGSD thetaStat emits θπ at four scales (in 02_heterozygosity/03_theta/): win5000.step1000, win10000.step2000, win50000.step10000 (multiscale) and win500000.step500000 (main). The chromosome-wide scrubber locks to win50000.step10000 for three reasons:
scale
windows / chr
sim_mat size
θπ noise floor
verdict
win5000.step1000
~33 000
~4.4 GB
~50–200 sites/win → noise
infeasible in browser
win10000.step2000
~16 500
~1.1 GB
marginal
infeasible in browser
win50000.step10000
~3 300
~44 MB
~500–2 000 sites/win → stable
DEFAULT
win500000.step500000
~66
trivial
very stable
too coarse for breakpoints
Browser memory: sim_mat is n_windows². Page 1 already handles ~5 000 windows ≈ 100 M cells; doubling that crashes most browsers. Below 50 kb, the matrix exceeds 1 GB.
Per-window θπ stability: at 9× coverage with ~10–40 sites/kb of callable density, a 5 kb window has 50–200 callable sites — the per-sample θπ variance is large enough that local-PCA on those values mostly captures noise. At 50 kb you have ~500–2 000 sites per window; the per-sample estimates are stable enough to PCA meaningfully. Below ~30 kb you embed noise, not biology.
Why finer step doesn't buy you breakpoint resolution: page 1's breakpoints are sharp because the local-PCA clustering signal is binary (in/out of inversion). The θπ analog is a diversity gradient across the inversion's flanks, not a step. Even at 1 kb step, the θπ-PCA breakpoint is diffuse over 20–50 kb of genomic space — the underlying biological gradient sets the floor. The finer scales (5/10 kb) are still useful for per-candidate breakpoint inspection (planned, post-v4.0 boundary panel) but not for the chromosome-wide scrubber.
What's shipped now (v3.99 t14e+ continue)
Structural scaffold only — this empty-state, the tab button, layer detection for the three new layers, schema documentation in §22.
Coming in follow-up turns: the six panel renderers (largely reusing page 1's draw functions parameterized by which layer to read), the cross-method candidate-list merge in the catalogue (dosage-only / θπ-only / both / both_overlap_partial), and the per-candidate concordance sub-view (the figure mockups A-F) on the candidate-focus page.
Blocking: the three R-side scripts. STEP_R39 packages STEP_Q05_aggregate_theta.sh's theta.<CHR>.win50000.step10000.tsv.gz (per-sample θπ at 50 kb / 10 kb step from ANGSD pestPG) into the theta_pi_per_window JSON layer; STEP_R40 runs local PCA on those θπ profiles per window with the same neighbourhood as page 1's dosage local PCA; STEP_R41 thresholds the diversity-|Z| into L1 envelopes, re-decomposes inside each, promotes L2s passing thresholds.

Local PCA scrubber on GHSL — phased haplotype similarity stream

Third of the four discovery streams (dosage · θπ · GHSL · Q). Mirrors the page 1 pipeline (per-window analysis → window×window similarity → MDS → cluster → candidate intervals) but driven by Group-level Haplotype Similarity by Locus instead of dosage or θπ.

Drag-drop <chr>_phase2_ghsl.json on this atlas to populate. Layers marked ⚪ activate the panels they feed; the rest stay in empty-state. Click the green schema badge in the header for the full registry of all known layers.
Panel layout (mirrors page 1 / page 12)
When the GHSL layers load, this page will render the same six-panel layout as page 1 and page 12: (top) per-window |Z| waveform with cand / L1 / L2 / win zone bars; (middle) per-sample lines colored by GHSL K-cluster assignment, with PC1 / PC2 toggle; (left) sim_mat heatmap of GHSL window×window similarity (multi-scale: nn40 → nn320, default nn320); (right) K-means scatter on PC1 / PC2 of the GHSL local-PCA loadings; (bottom) L3 contingency tables — same recluster + Hungarian-aligned coloring as page 1.
No candidate selected.
Promote an interval to the candidate list, then open it here. Two paths:
— page 4 catalogue: select an L2 row (or L1-merged group) → 👁 view as candidate, or
— page 1 diagnostic: lock colors on an L2 → ⬆ promote to candidate.
Promoted candidates start provisional; flip mark confirmed here once reviewed.
📷 gallery 0 selected of 0
Load a JSON to populate the catalogue.
Negative regions complement of the catalogue · multi-layer evidence battery
⚠
What "no detectable inversion" means — and what it does not
You can never prove no inversions in a region with 100% certainty. What this page shows is regions where no inversion signal was detected above the study’s resolution, sample size, and callability limits. Tiny inversions, very rare inversions (carried by 1–2 fish), inversions in repetitive or low-callability regions, and inversions whose breakpoints fall outside short-read mappability can all be missed. The region_status field is the right level of claim (e.g. no_detectable_inversion_high_confidence), not a binary positive / negative.
region_status — the five values
vocabulary
value
color
meaning
inversion_candidate
positive
Positive call from the catalogue — cross-listed here for completeness.
complex_candidate
complex
Multi-arrangement / overlapping calls — evidence is consistent with rearrangement but the structure is not a simple inversion.
no_detectable_inversion_high_confidence
high-conf neg
Region passes all evidence layers cleanly: high callable coverage, sufficient SNP density, no local-PCA bands, no GHSL block, no SV support, no abnormal heterozygosity.
no_detectable_inversion_low_power
low-power neg
No inversion detected, but power was limited: low SNP density, repetitive / low-mappability sequence, low coverage, assembly gaps, or missing data.
not_testable
not testable
Region cannot be assessed at all — e.g. assembly gap, fully masked, complete dropout. Do not interpret.
Evidence layers
multi-layer battery
Each region is evaluated against the following evidence layers. A high-confidence negative passes all of them; a low-power negative may have one or more layers in a "limited" state.
layer
expected pattern if no inversion
Local PCA / MDS
no 3-band or multi-band inversion structure
GHSL
no stable high/low haplotype-divergence block
SV callers
no supported INV / BND breakpoint evidence (DELLY2 + Manta)
Heterozygosity
no inversion-like heterozygote excess block
LD / haplotype structure
no long divergent haplotype block (when phased data available)
Synteny
no internal rearrangement signal in cross-species alignment (when applicable)
Read depth / mapping
no suspicious coverage or mapping artifact
Callable mask
region is well covered and analyzable
Region inventory
populated when JSON loads
Upload format — negative_regions.json
region schema
One row per region. chr / start_bp / end_bp define the interval. region_status is one of the five values above. evidence is an object with one boolean (or "limited") per layer. notes and citation are optional. 
{
  "metadata": { "panel_name": "negative regions — LG28 sweep", "date": "..." },
  "regions": [
    { "region_id": "LG28:0-1.5Mb",
      "chr": "LG28",
      "start_bp": 0,
      "end_bp": 1500000,
      "region_status": "no_detectable_inversion_high_confidence",
      "evidence": {
        "local_pca": "pass",
        "ghsl":      "pass",
        "sv_callers": "pass",
        "heterozygosity": "pass",
        "ld":        "pass",
        "callable_mask": "pass"
      },
      "snp_density": 0.012,
      "callable_fraction": 0.94,
      "n_samples": 226,
      "notes": "stable, high-coverage region; manuscript Fig. S3 control" },
    { "region_id": "LG28:5.0-7.5Mb",
      "chr": "LG28",
      "start_bp": 5000000,
      "end_bp": 7500000,
      "region_status": "no_detectable_inversion_low_power",
      "evidence": {
        "local_pca": "pass",
        "ghsl":      "limited",
        "sv_callers": "pass",
        "heterozygosity": "pass",
        "callable_mask": "limited"
      },
      "snp_density": 0.0035,
      "callable_fraction": 0.62,
      "notes": "repetitive, low SNP density — absence call carries reduced confidence" }
  ]
}
TSV variant accepted with header region_id · chr · start_bp · end_bp · region_status (optional snp_density, callable_fraction, n_samples, notes, citation).
Methods note
manuscript-ready
Suggested manuscript wording (Methods):
We classified regions as inversion-negative when no candidate was detected across all evidence layers (local PCA, GHSL, SV calls, heterozygosity-block, haplotype-divergence) given the study’s resolution of [X SNPs / window, N samples] and the available callable mask. Regions without inversion candidates were not interpreted as definitively inversion-free; rather, they were classified as showing no detectable polymorphic inversion signal under the resolution, sample size, and callability limits of our multi-layer framework.
Or shorter (Discussion):
Absence calls indicate no detectable inversion signal in our cohort, not proof of absolute inversion absence.

No active candidate.

— page 1 (lock colors → ⬆ promote to candidate), or
— page 4 catalogue (👁 view as candidate).

Load a precomp JSON to view the popstats stack.

windows summary:
Load a precomp JSON to view the per-window summary.
per-window strip · color = |Z| · click a tick to jump · L1/L2 zone bars on top
biallelic SNPs called from genotype likelihoods (low-coverage WGS).
ANGSD biSNP discovery — C. gariepinus 226-sample hatchery cohort
SNP discovery on per-chromosome chunks of fClaHyb_Gar_LG.fa (28 pseudochromosomes, 963.9 Mb). Computed from BAMs filtered through MODULE_1 QC (depth, mapping rate, t-len). Per-chromosome chunked array runs (S06_run_bisnp_discovery_chunks.slurm), output as .mafs.gz for downstream BEAGLE imputation + PCA.
Parameters (MODULE_2A canonical, applied uniformly across all 28 chromosomes):
-GL 1SAMtools genotype-likelihood model — standard for low-coverage WGS
-minQ 25Minimum base quality
-minMapQ 25Minimum mapping quality
-baq 1BAQ recalibration
-C 50Excessive-mismatch downweight
-setMinDepthInd 3Min per-individual depth (from MODULE_1 QC distribution)
-setMaxDepthInd 57Max per-individual depth (from MODULE_1 QC distribution)
-minInd 200Min individuals with data per site (200/226 = 88%)
-SNP_pval 1e-6SNP significance threshold
-minMaf 0.05Minimum minor allele frequency
-pestFolded SFS prior (no outgroup; F₁ hybrid hatchery violates HWE — folded by design)
For other cohorts, the precomp export script (planned, schema 2.15 §8.x) emits these parameters as a top-level angsd_bisnp_params object so this panel can read them generically. This particular tooltip text is hard-coded for the gariepinus project's known canonical run.
filter by L2: min |Z|:
w# center Mb n bi-SNPs span kb bi-SNPs / kb |Z| λ₁ λ₂ λ₁/λ₂ L1 L2
— / — confirmed candidates · ←/→ to navigate · uncheck "mark confirmed" on page 2 to remove
No confirmed candidates yet.
Mark candidates as confirmed using the checkbox on page 2 candidate focus. Confirmed candidates appear here in a carousel — useful for stepping through the final inversion set for manuscript figures.
annotation cockpit cursor: — Mb · no candidate — candidates ←/→ step cursor · Shift+←/→ jump 5 · click canvas to jump · Esc to clear
No candidates on this chromosome yet.
Promote candidates from page 1 (lock colors → promote) or page 4 (catalogue → view as candidate). Then return here to walk through the chromosome.
Move the cursor over a candidate to see its info here.
Band selection and annotation come in next iteration.

Marker panels

(no marker layer loaded)
Diagnostic PCR marker panels for each candidate inversion regime. Panels reproduce the genome-based regime call (g0 / g1 / g2 from fish_regime_calls.tsv) using 3–10 markers per candidate. See SCHEMA §10 for column contracts. Activates when marker_panel_summary is in _layers_present.

Boundary zone refinement

Refines promoted candidates into approximate left/right boundary zones. Hotkeys: E override left, F override right, A accept auto, B save, R reset.

Cross-species breakpoints

Catalogue of chromosome-scale rearrangements between Clarias gariepinus and C. macrocephalus (wfmash 1-to-1 alignment). Each breakpoint is annotated with the flanking repeat-element density on both species — ● Spalax-style all_TE enrichment flagged in the catalogue. Drop a cs_breakpoints_v1.json file to populate. Hotkeys: ↑/↓ step through catalogue, I/T/F filter inversion / translocation / fission, Esc clear filters. · ⓘ classification framework
No cross-species breakpoints loaded.
Generate a cs_breakpoints_v1.json by running STEP_CS01_extract_breakpoints.py on a wfmash 1-to-1 PAF of the Cgar and Cmac haplotypes (see cross_species_breakpoints/README.md in the LANTA pipeline tarball), then drag it into this window. The atlas reuses the per-chrom *_repeat_density_TEfull.json files already loaded by the boundaries page for the flanking density panels — both species' TE JSONs need to be loaded for the comparison panels to populate.

Multi-species classification cockpit

Place each Cgar↔Cmac breakpoint on the catfish phylogeny. Click a species in the tree to see how its homologous region compares — chromosome context, orientation, boundary status. Auto-suggests architecture class (A–F) from the lineage distribution. · ⓘ classification framework
No active breakpoint.
Select a breakpoint on page 13 cross-species first (or load a cs_breakpoints_v1.json), then return to this page. Optional: drop a synteny_multispecies_v1.json and/or phylo_tree_v1.json to populate the comparative analysis. Until then, a default 9-species reference tree is shown for navigation.
Click a species in the tree to see its homologous region detail here.

Statistical profile of inversion-associated genomic features

Comparative summary of breakpoint context, genomic composition, functional cargo, population variation, and breeding-relevant burden across parental catfish genomes.
To determine whether inversion candidates represent random structural variation or biologically distinct genomic units, this page quantifies their breakpoint context, repeat environment, gene cargo, population-genetic properties, and cross-species orthologous context. Each statistic is compared against chromosome- and size-matched genomic backgrounds where possible. Population-based tests are performed in the African catfish broodstock panel, whereas bighead catfish values represent orthologous genomic context unless population resequencing data are available.

Marker readiness panel

Confidence-tiered candidate markers for structural-haplotype genotyping in Clarias gariepinus broodstock breeding programmes.
Each promoted inversion candidate is assigned to one of four confidence tiers based on per-variant allele frequencies in three karyotype groups (STD/STD, STD/INV, INV/INV) and on the availability of positive/negative controls. Tier 1 is a private indel/SNP tag with clean dosage (AF_STD ≤ 0.02, AF_HET ∈ [0.25, 0.75], AF_INV ≥ 0.80) AND identified controls in all three groups AND bighead specificity confirmed. Tier 2 is a multi-marker panel (≥3 linked private markers) or a single strong tag with imperfect dosage. Tier 3 is a breakpoint PCR candidate awaiting wet-lab validation — demoted from prior architecture because breakpoint precision is uncertain. Tier 4 is exploratory only. Because the target breeding system involves interspecific F₁ hybrids, candidate markers are additionally screened against the bighead catfish orthologous genome to distinguish African inversion tags from maternal-species sequence differences. Candidate markers are provided as a starting resource and should be locally validated before routine breeding deployment.

Quick reference

Tabs

1 local PCA |z|Main scrubber — sim_mat, robust |Z|, pop-stat tracks, K-means PCA, L3 contingency. Where you do exploratory inversion calling on the dosage signal — tune K, lock colors, draft + commit candidates.
2 local PCA θπSame scrubber, but on per-sample θπ (nucleotide diversity) instead of dosage. Orthogonal validation of inversion candidates + discovery of regions only the diversity signal sees (sweeps, balancing selection). Empty until R-side ships the θπ layers (STEP_R39/R40/R41). SCHEMA §22.
3 candidate focusDeep-dive on a single promoted candidate. Multi-panel grid (sim_mini, karyogram, dosage heatmap, ancestry strip), prev/next navigation. Toggle ⭐ mark confirmed here to populate the confirmed-walkthrough tab. Two-track candidates show per-track regime chips here (turn 48).
4 boundariesRefine each promoted candidate's [start_bp, end_bp] into approximate left/right boundary zones using SV anchors + flanking PC1. Auto-propose + manual override (E/F at cursor). SCHEMA §12. Edits persist across reloads (turn 51).
5 catalogueSortable, filterable catalogue of all L2 envelopes (or L1-merged regions). Hover any column header for the definition. Export selected rows as TSV or Markdown. 📋 export registry ships per-track-aware TSV+JSON for downstream cluster pipeline (turn 45). 📝 manuscript bundle ships an LLM-ready Markdown + companion TSV (turn 50).
6 negative regionsInversion-negative inventory — the complement of the catalogue. Each row is a genomic interval evaluated against the multi-layer evidence battery (local PCA, GHSL, SV calls, heterozygosity, callable mask, etc.) and assigned a region_status (inversion_candidate / complex_candidate / no_detectable_inversion_high_confidence / no_detectable_inversion_low_power / not_testable). Caution banner + Methods note make the manuscript-grade framing explicit: "no detectable inversion under defined resolution", never "no inversions exist". Empty until a negative_regions.json is loaded.
7 karyotype / tierTwo views in one page. Karyotype — per-promoted-candidate sample-level regime breakdown. Tier — 14-axis classification view (existence A-D, boundaries, groups, internal structure, mechanism, age, burden, tier). Tier renders empty until the cluster-side final_classification.json layer is loaded via 📦 load registry. Two-track candidates show a 7-column karyotype with per-track membership (turn 43).
8 popstatsStack of population-genetic tracks (Z, SNP density, BEAGLE uncertainty, coverage, θπ, FST, Hobs/Hexp, ancestry Δ12) aligned to the chromosome. Click chips to toggle visibility.
9 ancestryPer-window ancestry view: K-cluster assignment + delta12 confidence heatmap, sample × window. Driven by the ancestry layer in precomp.
10 windowsPer-window summary table — robust |Z|, λ₁/λ₂, eigenvalue ratio, SNP counts, L1/L2 envelope membership. Click any row to jump to that window. Coloring follows whichever sortable column you click (one click sets sort + color).
11 confirmedCarousel walk-through of all candidates marked confirmed on page 3. ←/→ to step through — useful for assembling manuscript figures from the final inversion set.
12 markersDiagnostic PCR marker panels per candidate (3–10 markers tagging each K=3 regime). Hidden until phase-13 marker JSONs are loaded. SCHEMA §10.
13 cross-speciesCross-species breakpoint catalogue (Cgar↔Cmac wfmash 1-to-1 alignment). Each chromosome-scale rearrangement (inversion / translocation / fission/fusion) is shown with both species' coordinates and the flanking repeat-element density on both sides. ● flag in catalogue = Spalax-style all_TE enrichment at the breakpoint flank (≥1.5× chrom mean). ✪ flag = the cross-species breakpoint coincides with a polymorphic inversion candidate from the 226-sample cohort (the same locus is recurrently rearrangement-prone across timescales). Empty until a cs_breakpoints_v1.json file is loaded — output of STEP_CS01_extract_breakpoints.py in the cross_species_breakpoints/ LANTA pipeline. Hotkeys: ↑/↓ step, I/T/F event-type filters, Esc clear filters. Manuscript hook for repeat-mediated chromosome-evolution mechanism (Kuang et al. 2024-style).
14 stats profileStatistical profile of inversion-associated genomic features. One comparative inversion-phenotype synthesis table covering breakpoint architecture (distance to synteny edges, fusion/fission proximity), genomic composition (repeat density, telomere/centromere/gap proximity, gene density), functional cargo (GO/KEGG, breeding-candidate genes), population variation (heterozygosity, ROH overlap, deleterious burden, FST between haplotypes), and breeding utility (markerability), with side-by-side African vs Bighead columns. Auto-derives rows from state.crossSpecies (page 13) + state.candidateList (pages 1-3); rows that need annotation tracks (gene density, GO/KEGG, ROH BED, deleterious-variant calls, FST) accept a stats_profile.json or .tsv overlay via the load JSON / TSV button. View modes: detailed (6 columns) / compact (4 columns). Filters: by category, scope (all / African-only / cross-species), significant-only. Export: CSV (current view) + SVG (manuscript figure). Manuscript synthesis: "What is statistically special about inversion regions, and are the patterns shared between parental species?"
15 marker panelMarker readiness panel — per-inversion confidence tier table built around private indels with clean dosage signatures, not breakpoint PCR. Four tiers: Tier 1 private indel/SNP tag (AF_STD ≤0.02, AF_HET ∈ [0.25, 0.75], AF_INV ≥0.80) with positive/negative controls in all three karyotype groups AND bighead specificity confirmed; Tier 2 multi-marker panel (≥3 linked private markers) or strong tag (AF_STD ≤0.05, AF_INV ≥0.70) with controls but imperfect het; Tier 3 breakpoint PCR candidate (DEMOTED — breakpoint precision is uncertain even on the cs_breakpoint catalogue); Tier 4 exploratory only. AF scoring runs live in the browser from a variant_afs.json upload (per-variant AF in STD/HET/INV groups). Atlas auto-suggests positive (INV/INV, HET) and negative (STD/STD) control samples from candidate karyotype state on page 1. Includes the 5-step pilot validation checklist with cross-species controls (bighead negative, F₁ hybrid expected pattern, no-template) and the protective methods sentence: "Because the target breeding system involves interspecific F₁ hybrids, candidate markers were additionally screened against the bighead catfish orthologous genome to distinguish African inversion tags from maternal-species sequence differences." Accepts marker_panel.json overlay with primer sequences, amplicon sizes, and validation status.
16 helpThis page.
17 focal-vs-bg widgetSpalax-style focal-vs-background distribution comparison. Renders on page 11 (boundaries — focal = candidate scan range, radius shared with the parent toolbar) and page 16 (cross-species — focal = breakpoint ±2 Mb, independent radius). Wilcoxon rank-sum P value (live, in-browser, normal approximation w/ continuity correction). Metric pool auto-discovers from loaded layers: |Z| (always available, from the cohort precomp), F_ST/Hobs/θπ (when popstats live server is hooked up), tRNA_all / rRNA_all / rRNA_5S / ncRNA_all (when the ncRNA density layer is loaded), all_TE (when the repeat-density layer is loaded). Z-transform toggle on by default. Bg scope: per-chrom (default) or genome-wide-excl-focal. View mode: full or zoomed-to-scan-range. Note: on page 16, the metric values come from the 226-fish cohort (single haplotype), not from cross-species data — so ‘|Z|’ here means “cohort divergence at this position”, not “cross-species divergence”. The widget hides with a hint when the active breakpoint’s gar_chr doesn’t match the cohort chrom currently loaded on page 1.

Hotkeys / tutorial

Inversion Atlas — keyboard workflow (click to toggle)

In the Inversion Atlas:

  • Use ← / → to navigate the genome (step between L2 windows).
  • Click Zoom L2 or Window to change the scale.
  • Use ↑ (arrow up) to validate a merge.
  • Use ↓ (arrow down) to demote.
  • Click promote to add the consolidated interval to the candidate list.
  • Then go to the catalogue page for results.

That's the core L1 → L2 review loop. Other hotkeys are documented per-tab in the row descriptions above.

Breakpoint classification framework

Two-layer classification for cross-species breakpoints (click to toggle)

Cross-species breakpoints (page 13) and inversion candidates (pages 1, 5) are classified along two independent axes. The architecture class (Layer 1) describes what kind of breakpoint it is. The age model (Layer 2) describes when the rearrangement most likely happened. Both axes use the evidence already in the atlas (TE flanking density, local PCA bands, synteny block edges, dXY) plus future layers (10-species synteny, miniprot anchors) when they ship.

Layer 1 — Architecture

A Simple inversion Two clean inversion edges. Local PCA / MDS bands support inversion. No strong synteny boundary with related species. Ordinary polymorphic inversion.
B Synteny-boundary inversion Inversion edge overlaps the edge of a conserved syntenic block. Same block boundary exists in related species, but chromosome identity is mostly conserved. The inversion may reuse a pre-existing genomic boundary.
C Fusion/fission-associated One inversion breakpoint overlaps a cross-species chromosome split/join point. One species has the region on one chromosome; another species has homologous material split across two chromosomes. The inversion may be anchored at an older karyotype breakpoint.
D Terminal translocation / reversed attachment Region is near a chromosome end. Homologous segment appears on a different chromosome in another species, possibly in reversed orientation. Synteny suggests terminal segment movement. A chromosome-end segment may have moved or reattached in another genomic context.
E Recurrent rearrangement hotspot The same region carries inversion AND translocation/fusion/fission AND orientation-switch signals — plus repeat-rich / low-mappability / telomeric / centromeric features. Multiple species may show different rearrangements around the same point. This region may repeatedly break and repair in different ways. Scientific label: recurrent breakage-and-repair hotspot.
F Ambiguous / assembly-risk Gap near breakpoint, low mappability, collapsed repeat, scaffold join, weak SV support, only one breakpoint visible. Do not over-interpret yet.

Layer 2 — Age model

YOUNG-POP Inversion only seen inside the focal population. Low dXY between standard / inverted haplotypes. Long shared haplotype around breakpoint. No evidence in related species. The inversion is likely recent.
OLD-POLY Strong dXY elevation inside the inversion. Deep standard-vs-inverted haplotype split. Arrangement-specific phylogeny. Possibly shared across populations / species. The inversion itself may be old.
OLD-BP / YOUNG-INV Inversion is polymorphic only now / recently, but its breakpoint overlaps an ancient synteny / karyotype / repeat boundary. dXY inside the inversion is not extremely old. The fragile region is old, but the inversion using it may be younger.
LINEAGE-KARYO Breakpoint explains a difference between species' chromosomes. Outgroup suggests the event happened on one lineage. Fusion / fission / translocation is fixed between species. Mostly a species-evolution breakpoint, not a population inversion.
MULTI-AGE-HOTSPOT Breakpoint substrate appears ancient. Karyotype change happened at one time. Present-day inversion / translocation polymorphism may be younger. Same region shows several rearrangement types. Different rearrangement events of different ages reused the same fragile region.

Combined label format

candidate_id | architecture | age_model | confidence

Example: LG28_candidate_X | Class D/E (terminal translocation + recurrent hotspot) | OLD-BP/YOUNG-INV | medium

Manuscript framing

Some present-day inversion boundaries were classified as recurrent rearrangement hotspots when they colocalized with cross-species karyotype breakpoints, terminal translocation signals, and orientation-discordant synteny. These cases were interpreted as old breakpoint regions potentially reused by younger inversion events.

Status: this is a reference framework. Per-breakpoint classification UI (assign a class + age tag to each breakpoint, persist across sessions, auto-suggest from evidence layers) is scoped for a future session — see TURN 115+ in the handoff. The reference here documents the schema so manuscript text and reviewer responses use consistent vocabulary.

Changelog

Recent additions (click to toggle)
v4 turn 82Negative regions page shipped (page 6, between Catalogue and Karyotype/tier). Inversion-negative inventory — the complement of the catalogue. Each row is a genomic interval evaluated against the multi-layer evidence battery (local PCA, GHSL, SV calls, heterozygosity, callable mask, etc.) with one of five region_status values: inversion_candidate / complex_candidate / no_detectable_inversion_high_confidence / no_detectable_inversion_low_power / not_testable. Caution banner + Methods note both make the manuscript-grade framing explicit: "no detectable inversion under defined resolution", never "no inversions exist". Loads negative_regions.json. Downstream tabs renumbered: karyotype 7, popstats 8, ancestry 9, windows 10, confirmed 11, markers 12, cross-species 13, stats profile 14, marker panel 15, help 16.
v4 turn 80Workflow stage bands on the tab bar (very subtle 2px top border per tab, color-coded by stage: discovery / refinement / classification / synthesis / help). First-use attention pulse on five key affordances: load JSON, 🔬 Zoom L2, 📊 Window step cycler, ▤ move-to-minimap, ⬆ promote to candidate. Pulse dismissal persists in localStorage so returning users see a clean UI.
v4 turn 79Cross-atlas hash routing. Inversion_atlas.html#page18 now lands on the marker panel directly (was: opened on whatever tab was last visited). hashchange events also fire for in-page navigation. The marker panel methods note now includes a back-link to Population_atlas.html#page8 (breeding highlights).
v4 turn 78Marker panel rebuilt around private indels. Tier 1 = clean dosage signature (AF_STD ≤0.02, AF_HET ∈ [0.25, 0.75], AF_INV ≥0.80) + controls + bighead specificity; Tier 3 demoted (breakpoint precision uncertain). Live AF scoring from variant_afs.json. Auto-suggested controls from karyotype. (Page 14.)
v4 turn 77Stats profile page added (page 13). Comparative inversion-phenotype synthesis table covering breakpoint architecture, genomic composition, functional cargo, population variation, and breeding utility. Auto-derives from atlas state; accepts stats_profile.json overlay.
v4 turn 76Cross-species page gained macro-synteny layer (3-tier Sankey, length-matched permutation test, per-chrom relationship table). Schema bumped from v1 to v2 with synteny_blocks, chrom_lengths_query/target. (Page 12.)
v4 turn 75Cross-species breakpoints page shipped (page 12). Driven by STEP_CS01_extract_breakpoints.py + wfmash 1-to-1 alignment. Per-breakpoint event_type (inversion / translocation / fission/fusion), repeat-element flank density, recurrence flag against 226-sample candidates.

New JSON schemas

File formats accepted by recent pages (click to toggle)
cs_breakpoints_v1.jsonPage 12 input. Output of STEP_CS01_extract_breakpoints.py. Contains breakpoints[] (per-breakpoint event_type, gar/mac coordinates, repeat-element flank density), synteny_blocks[] (1-to-1 anchor blocks for the macro-synteny layer), chrom_lengths_query/target, and metadata. Schema v2.
dotplot_mashmap_v1.jsonOptional page-16 input for the dot plot panel. Multi-resolution mashmap output (segment-length × percent-identity grid, all --one-to-one). Generated once on the cluster by STEP_DP01_run_mashmap_grid.sh + STEP_DP02_aggregate_mashmap_to_json.py. Schema: resolutions[] with label, segment_length_bp, pi_min, n_alignments, alignments[]. The dot plot also renders from cs_breakpoints_v1 v2 synteny_blocks[] alone — the mashmap layer is additive, providing the resolution dropdown for finer / coarser views.
<chrom>_ncrna_density.jsonPage 11 input. Optional sibling track to the repeat-density layer. Per-chrom density (loci/Mb) for tRNA (tRNAscan-SE), rRNA (barrnap), and ncRNA-other (Rfam/Infernal). Sub-classes preserved: tRNA_HC/tRNA_pseudo/tRNA_intronic/tRNA_Sec; rRNA_5S/5_8S/18S/28S/partial; ncRNA_snRNA/snoRNA/miRNA/other. Generated once per haplotype by aggregate_ncrna_density.R from the canonical fClaHyb_*.tRNA.gff3 + .rRNA.gff3 + .ncRNA.gff3. Schema: tool: 'ncrna_density_v1', chromosomes[] with by_class mirroring the TEfull shape exactly so downstream consumers (focal-vs-bg widget) auto-discover the metrics.
stats_profile.jsonPage 13 overlay. Optional. Adds rows the atlas can't compute itself — gene density, GO/KEGG enrichment, ROH overlap, deleterious burden, FST between haplotypes. Each row: { category, label, scope, african_result, bighead_result, interpretation }. TSV variant accepted.
variant_afs.jsonPage 14 input for the AF-based tier promotion. Shape: { metadata, variants_by_inversion: { <inv_id>: [<variant>] } }. Each variant: { chr, pos, ref, alt, type, indel_size_bp, af_std, af_het, af_inv }. Atlas computes private_score, dosage_score, final_score, gel-visibility, and assigns Tier 1–4.
marker_panel.jsonPage 14 overlay. User-curated overrides with primer sequences, expected amplicon sizes, validation status, and per-marker controls block (positive_controls_INV / heterozygote_controls / negative_controls_STD / cross-species fields).

Vocabulary

L1 / L2 / L3Coarse → fine PCA window scales. L2 envelopes are what the scrubber clusters and what the catalogue rows correspond to. L3 cuts let you split a single L2 into sub-intervals at commit time without re-running the upstream local PCA.
Window resolutionA candidate is committed at either L2 resolution (the default — boundaries snap to L2-envelope edges) or W resolution (per-window precision; boundaries can fall mid-L2). Set on commit; round-trips in JSON. SCHEMA §24.
L3 cutA per-window cut inside a candidate's draft, applied at commit time to split the candidate into N sub-intervals (a SPLIT). Each sub-interval is a sibling of the others, linked via parent_split_id. SCHEMA §24.
IntervalA contiguous span of L2s that you might want to treat as one unit. Drafted in candidate mode on tab 1 (arrow-up extends, arrow-down shrinks; F flip, B set boundary, C cut) or assembled by L1-merging in the catalogue.
CandidateAn interval that has been promoted via ⬆ promote to candidate (tab 1) or 👁 view as candidate (tab 5). Lives on the candidate list in the right sidebar. Starts provisional; flip ⭐ mark confirmed on tab 3 once reviewed. Lane-stacking on the cand-strip resolves overlapping candidates visually. SCHEMA §25.
RegimeWhat each fish (sample) is assigned to within a candidate. For K=3: g0 / g1 / g2 = homo1 / het / homo2 (PC1-rank order). Fish-regime calls export to fish_regime_calls.tsv (per-track since turn 46).
Regime registryUser-curated annotation of "this group of L2s belongs to inversion-system X." Each regime carries a label, axis_topology (SCHEMA §26), L2 members, candidate members (track-scoped since turn 48), and notes. Open via the ⚙ registry button on tab 3's regime row.
Axis topologyRegime-registry vocabulary controlling how a regime aligns to the genome's principal axes: biallelic_inversion, nested_inversion, polymorphism_complex, family_LD, diversity_only, unspecified. User-chosen, never auto-assigned. SCHEMA §26.
TrackA subset of a candidate's K-bands grouped as a single inversion call. Single-track is the default (one track owns all bands). Two-track candidates split bands into two parallel inversion calls sharing the genomic region — common when K≥4 and the σ-profile says TWO_INVERSIONS. Each track has its own active_bands, regime_id, and per-track sample membership. Mutually exclusive: each band belongs to at most one track. (Turns 36-48.)
Atlas trioThree sibling lenses on the same loaded JSON. Inversion Atlas (tabs 1–11) is the curation tool — drafts candidates, manages tracks, refines boundaries. Diversity Atlas (tab 13, SCHEMA §17 + §23) reinterprets the same data through a population-genetic-diversity lens. Genome Atlas (tab 14, SCHEMA §28) grounds both in the reference assembly + annotations. Inversion Atlas computes (you curate); the other two are render-only — they consume layers produced by the cluster-side R/genome pipelines.
Boundary zoneAn approximate ±5-window range around an inferred breakpoint, refined on tab 4. SCHEMA §12 reserves the term exact breakpoint for junction-level evidence (split reads / assembly junctions) — most refinements are zone-level.
σ verdictFor K≥4 candidates, classifies what causes the extra bands. SCHEMA §27 vocab: TWO_INVERSIONS / CROSSOVER_ARTIFACTS / NOISY_REGION / NA / UNDETERMINED. See "σ verdicts" section below for definitions.
Local launcherThe atlas.sh / atlas.bat scripts shipped with the codebase. Start a local HTTP server bound to 127.0.0.1:8765 and open the atlas in your browser. Compared to opening the HTML via file://, the launcher gives you a stable localStorage origin so saved candidates / regime registry / boundary edits persist reliably across sessions. See atlas_launcher_README.md. (Turn 54.)
Label vocabulary [v4 turn 87]Two display vocabularies for K-means bands of a candidate, switchable via the labels toggle in the sidebar (legacy / detailed). Legacy is the default, neutral naming: band 1 (lo) / band 2 (mid) / band 3 (hi) for K=3 buttons, g0 / g1 / g2 in the legend, band 0 / band 1 / band 2 in karyotype-page pills. Makes no biological claim. Detailed is the operational H-system: H1/H1 / H1/H2 / H2/H2 for K=3, extending to H1/H3 / H2/H3 / H3/H3 for K=4..6. The H-labels are operational, ordered by median PC1, NOT confirmed biological assignments — see "Operational structural-haplotype framework" section below for caveats and manuscript-safe wording.

Operational structural-haplotype framework [v4 turn 87]

Local PCA bands are observed clusters, not biological genotypes. The mapping from band to genotype requires per-sample heterozygosity / haplotype-divergence evidence that local PCA alone cannot supply. The two display vocabularies (legacy / detailed, see Vocabulary table above) reflect this distinction: legacy labels make no claim; detailed labels offer an operational ordering by median PC1.

Why a single inversion gives 3 bands. A simple biallelic inversion has two haploid arrangements (call them H1 and H2). At the diploid individual level these produce three unordered classes: H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2 — three local-PCA bands. The traditional "REF / HET / INV" naming is replaced here by H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2 because local PCA does not directly prove which haplotype is reference-like or inverted; the H-system avoids that unsupported claim.

Why some candidates show 4–6 bands. A region may contain more than two major structural haplotypes. Three haplotypes (H1, H2, H3) produce up to six diploid combinations: H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2, H1/H3, H2/H3, H3/H3. Six bands does not require six orientations; it can mean three underlying haplotype classes producing six diploid combinations. Intermediate cases (4 or 5 visible bands) occur when one or more diploid combinations is rare, missing, or merged in the PCA projection.

How heterozygosity disambiguates. Local PCA alone cannot tell whether a band is H1/H1, H1/H2, etc. The disambiguating evidence is per-sample heterozygosity: homozygote-like classes should show low within-sample haplotype divergence, heterozygote-like classes should show elevated divergence. A band may also contain two hidden heterozygosity modes (e.g., 50% low-het + 50% high-het in the same PC band) — these need to be split into sub-bands. Per-sample heterozygosity is part of pages 2 (θπ) and is being added; until it ships, the detailed labels are operational only.

Manuscript-safe wording. When writing about candidates, prefer:

  • "candidate regions were classified into local structural-haplotype bands; bands were further interpreted as homozygote-like, heterozygote-like, mixed, or ambiguous based on within-sample haplotype divergence" — instead of "candidate inversions were classified as REF/REF, REF/INV, and INV/INV"
  • "six stable bands are consistent with a multi-haplotype structural system, potentially involving three major haplotype backgrounds and their diploid combinations" — instead of "six bands indicate multiple inversions"
  • "this band is H1/H1-like under an operational labeling scheme" — instead of "this band is AA"
  • "reference-like" or "inversion-like" only if independently supported by orientation / breakpoint / long-read evidence — never as a default

Caveats per band-count interpretation.

3 bandsLikely simple biallelic system: H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2.
4 bandsCould be a biallelic system plus one split class, one missing/merged class from a 3-haplotype system, asymmetry due to sample frequency, or one hidden axis not fully separated.
5 bandsCould be 3 homozygote-like + 2 heterozygote-like classes, with one expected heterozygote class missing, rare, selected against, or merged.
6 bandsCould be the full 3-haplotype diploid system: H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2, H1/H3, H2/H3, H3/H3.
No bandsNo strong local structural haplotype signal, too little variation, or insufficient local contrast.

Band order in PC1×PC2 space need not follow a biologically clean sequence. PCA is a projection — observed band order can be (e.g.) H1/H1, H1/H2, H2/H2, H1/H3 even though biologically H2/H3 might be expected to fall between H2/H2 and H3/H3. The detailed labels assigned by the resolver use the most parsimonious sub-pattern from the 3-haplotype model, ordered by median PC1; sub-pattern correctness has to be confirmed against heterozygosity evidence.

Manuscript vocabulary [v4 turn 100]

We use a tiered vocabulary that separates what is observed from what is inferred. Local PCA produces stable banding patterns at certain genomic positions — that is the observation. The inference is that those banding patterns reflect segregating haplotype classes. The further inference that those classes correspond to inversion orientations requires independent evidence and is reserved for the strongest cases.

structural-haplotype system Default term — a region where individuals segregate into stable local haplotype classes across adjacent windows. Conservative; makes no orientation claim. Use this whenever stable band assignments are observed but breakpoint or orientation evidence is missing.
segregating structural-haplotype system Long form, used when emphasizing that the system is polymorphic (multiple haplotype classes coexisting in the cohort), as opposed to a fixed difference between species or populations.
inversion-like structural-haplotype system Stronger term — used when the system shows signatures consistent with an inversion (long-range LD block, recombination suppression, distinct PCA bands across many windows) but the breakpoints are not yet localized.
candidate polymorphic inversion Reserved for systems with breakpoint or orientation evidence (DELLY/Manta calls, long-read split reads, synteny breaks, etc.). Use only when the inversion claim is independently supported.
polymorphic inversion The strongest term — only when orientation is confirmed (e.g., long-read assembly of both arrangements, validated breakpoints). Almost never used in this manuscript without the "candidate" qualifier.

Single-paragraph methods statement (paste-ready):

"We first identified segregating structural-haplotype systems from stable local-PCA banding patterns. A subset of these systems showed inversion-like signatures and were further classified as candidate polymorphic inversions when independent breakpoint or orientation evidence was available. The term structural-haplotype system is used throughout as a conservative descriptor for regions with stable local haplotype segregation; the term inversion is reserved for candidates with supporting orientation or breakpoint evidence."

L1/L2/L3 hierarchy in the new vocabulary

The atlas's existing L1/L2/L3 envelope hierarchy maps cleanly onto the structural-haplotype framework:

L1 Broad segregating structural-haplotype region — a coarse chromosomal block where local PCA shows banded structure. May contain one or more L2 sub-blocks. The L1 envelope is the outer container; it does not yet imply a specific haplotype-class count or any inversion claim.
L2 Stable internal haplotype block — a sub-region of an L1 envelope where the K-means band assignments are stable (consistent across adjacent windows, low boundary signal). Each L2 has its own K, σ-verdict, and (in detailed mode) per-band heterozygosity profile. Multiple L2s within one L1 may share haplotype classes (in which case they merge under L1-merged catalogue view) or carry distinct ones.
L3 Refined candidate structural-haplotype interval — a further sub-block within an L2 where additional internal substructure is resolved (e.g., multimodal heterozygosity inside a band, sub-band splitting). L3 is the level at which detailed-mode candidates can have biologically distinct sub-haplotypes within a single PCA band.

The atlas's column "Regimes" annotates user-curated structural-haplotype systems, where one regime corresponds to one segregating structural-haplotype system that may span multiple L2 envelopes. The L1/L2/L3 hierarchy is the geometric subdivision; the regime registry is the biological-system annotation on top of it.

Band-reach + regime breadth [v4 turn 103-104]

Band-reach is the per-fish count of distinct K-means bands visited across a contiguous region. Fish that stay in 1–2 bands across many adjacent L2 envelopes are "lane-disciplined" — they are stable carriers of a particular structural-haplotype background. Fish that visit many bands either carry recombinant chromosomes (different haplotype backgrounds in different windows) or the local PCA has lost regime structure in that region.

Regime breadth is the per-region summary of band-reach values across all fish:

narrow ≥70% of fish have band-reach ≤2 across the windowed region. The cohort segregates cleanly into stable lanes — strong segregating-structural-haplotype-system signal. Many fish stay in a single band across the L2 chain, with the cohort distributed among multiple bands.
medium 30–70% of fish have band-reach ≤2. Mixed regime — partial segregation with some lane-crossing fish.
wide <30% of fish have band-reach ≤2. Most fish visit many bands. No clean structural-haplotype segregation in this region. Could mean: high recombination, small effective sample sizes per band, or low local-PCA discrimination.
no_signal Fewer than 2 bands populated (≥5 fish each). The region does not have the band structure needed to call regime breadth.

The regime toggle on the per-sample lines panel paints a thin colored strip above the plot showing per-L2 regime breadth (green=narrow, amber=medium, red=wide, grey=no_signal). On LG28, narrow regions on the left transition to wide regions on the right — this is the visible banding-discipline pattern that distinguishes segregating-structural-haplotype regions from non-segregating regions.

Hatchery applications [v4 turn 105]

Structural-haplotype systems are genomic units for broodstock management. They are not breeding recommendations on their own, but they define the units that breeding programs can track, preserve, and test for trait association.

Use cases:

1. Hidden-diversity surveillance A hatchery may know that fish A is from family 1 and fish B is from family 2, but not know that only 8 fish in the entire broodstock carry haplotype H3 at candidate region X. If those 8 fish are removed, that linked block disappears and may not be quickly recreated by recombination. The atlas surfaces this hidden layer.
2. Region-specific inbreeding Two fish may look unrelated by genome-wide FROH but be H1/H1 at a candidate region containing fertility, growth, or immune genes. Mating them creates offspring with high local homozygosity. Region-specific inbreeding is invisible to overall pedigree-based metrics.
3. Marker-assisted broodstock management Once candidate systems are catalogued, hatcheries can design cheap region-specific markers (KASP, amplicon sequencing, small SNP panels) and screen routine broodstock without re-running local PCA. The atlas → marker-design → routine-screen pipeline is the most direct practical value.
4. Trait-association units Structural-haplotype classes are better association units than individual SNPs because each class represents a whole inherited block. Future trait studies (growth, survival, deformity, fertility, disease resistance, hybrid offspring performance) test H1/H1 vs H1/H2 vs H2/H2 directly rather than millions of correlated SNPs.
5. Mating-design support (after validation) Once associations are validated, a simple decision table can guide mating. e.g.: "avoid H1/H1 × H1/H1 if that combination has low survival; prefer H1/H1 × H2/H2 if heterozygote advantage is documented." Validation is required — the atlas provides candidates, not breeding rules.

Manuscript-safe wording for the discussion section:

"These structural-haplotype systems are not immediately breeding recommendations, but they define genomic units that can be tracked, preserved, and tested for association with aquaculture traits. They reveal a hidden layer of broodstock diversity: stable linked haplotype backgrounds that may not be captured by family labels or genome-wide diversity summaries alone. A subset of these systems can be developed into cheap region-specific markers for routine broodstock screening, providing a practical foundation for marker-assisted broodstock management."

Why these regions matter for diversity preservation:

"These regions represent stable segregating structural-haplotype diversity: linked genomic backgrounds that are not rapidly broken down by recombination and may therefore be lost as entire blocks if their carriers are removed from the breeding population."

Key metrics

ConcordFraction of samples assigned to the same K-means group in two adjacent L2s after Hungarian alignment. High = L2s are interchangeable; suggests merge.
Cramér's VEffect-size measure of association between two clusterings. N-independent. <0.10 small, 0.10–0.30 medium, >0.30 large.
p-valueSignificance of the contingency. With persistent sample structure, p ≈ 0 is expected and confirms association — read together with concord.
ConservationMean concord with left + right L2 neighbors. Low (orange) = the L2 boundary is biologically real.
CoherenceCorrelation between two-half mean PC1 vectors within the L2. Low (red, <0.60) = INCOHERENT, suggests over-merge of distinct sub-regions.
Fam purityWithin-cluster fraction from the dominant family. ~1.0 (red, FAM-LD?) = clusters are family-pure → likely family LD, not real inversion. ~0.2 = real biological signal.
SilhouetteCluster separation quality from K-means. >0.5 good, <0.45 weak.
K usedThe number of clusters used for this L2. In adaptive mode, picked per-L2 by silhouette.
σ verdictFor K≥4 L2s, classifies the source of extra bands. SCHEMA §27.

σ verdicts (K ≥ 4 L2s) — SCHEMA §27

TWO_INVERSIONSAll samples have low σ (q50 < 0.05). Joint karyotypes are stable → two stacked inversions.
CROSSOVER_ARTIFACTSBimodal σ distribution with small high-σ tail (2–25% of samples drift). Suggests gene conversion / double crossovers in heterozygotes.
NOISY_REGIONEveryone has high σ. Low informative power.
NAK used ≤ 3. No extra bands to explain.
UNDETERMINEDProfile doesn't cleanly fit any pattern.

K labels TSV (for C++ pop-stats engine)

The export K labels TSV button in the sidebar produces one row per (L2, sample) with columns: chrom, l2_id, l2_idx, l2_start_bp, l2_end_bp, l2_n_windows, ind, cga, family_id, k_used, k_label, k_label_fixed, silhouette, fam_purity, coherence, cluster_ok.

k_label is the adaptive-K assignment (varies per L2 if adaptive mode is on); k_label_fixed is always at state.k (default 3) for cross-L2 comparisons. Group samples by k_label_fixed for uniform pop-stats; by k_label for per-L2 natural clustering.

Pop-stat tracks (--track_<label>)

Pass any per-window scalar TSV at export time:

--track_theta theta.tsv --track_fst_K1K3 fst.tsv --track_pi pi.tsv

TSV must have a value column plus one of:
(a) chrom, start_bp, end_bp, value (range form)
(b) chrom, window_idx, value (1-based index form)
(c) chrom, center_bp, value (center form)

Each track renders as a 60px stacked panel between the Z and PCA panels on page 1.

Color codes (cluster mode)

K-means group → blue / grey / orange / green / red (lo to hi PC1). Samples colored consistently within an L2; Hungarian-aligned to focal in mini-PCAs.

Lock button (🔒 lock colors to current L2) freezes labels from a reference L2 → samples keep those colors as you scrub through other L2s. Useful for tracking how a clean K=3 split holds up across boundaries.

Keyboard shortcuts

← / →Step one window
n / pJump to next / previous L1 or L2 (depending on travel mode)
SpaceToggle play / pause auto-scrub
While drafting a candidate (tab 1, candidate mode):
↑ / ↓Extend / shrink draft right edge
FFlip draft resolution between L2 and W (per-window)
BSet boundary at cursor (split point for SPLIT commit)
CCut draft at cursor — adds an L3 cut at this window
EnterCommit the draft (saves all sub-intervals from cuts as siblings linked by parent_split_id)
EscDiscard draft
While viewing a multi-track candidate (tab 3):
track 1 / 2 buttonsSwitch active track for band selection. Two-track candidates show track-1 and track-2 bands separately in regime row + karyotype.

Note on manuscript writing

Catalogue exports (TSV / Markdown) are designed to drop into Methods / Results sections. They're aids to writing — they don't write the manuscript. Nature acceptance depends on the underlying biology, the prose, and peer review.

Roadmap — what's shipped, what's coming

Tracking what's in flight across recent turns, what's pending in parallel chats, and what still needs designing. Each entry lists what it would take to actually build, so we don't lose the thread between sessions. Faded rows = shipped. Bold rows = active or next-up.

SHIPPED · turns 13–14e (current burst)
Block-presence chips + JSON inspector [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
20 chips per candidate · click to inspect

Page 2 candidate focus now shows a row of 20 chips at the top — one per known structured block file (existence A/B/C/D + boundary L/R + 12 other question blocks = 18 numbered + boundary_right sibling = 19 file types, plus the synthesis final_classification). Each chip:

  • Lit (filled) when that block is loaded for the active candidate; dim (outlined) when missing
  • Green = block contributed at least one axis to the 14-axis classification (per SCHEMA §21.6)
  • Blue = block loaded but supportive-only (no axis derivation: morphology, band_composition, block_detect, triangle_insulation, peel_diagnostic, synteny_dollo)
  • Gold = the synthesis block (final_classification), authoritative when present
  • Grey/dim = not loaded; tooltip explains how to load (drop structured/<block>.json via 📦 load registry on page 4)

Click a lit chip → toggles an inline JSON inspector below the chip row showing the raw block content (header fields like source_script, source_version, computed_at, groups_used.validation_level surfaced separately, then a scrollable <pre> with the full JSON). Click ✕ on the inspector or the chip again to close. Multiple inspectors can be open simultaneously. Blocks larger than 200 KB stringified are truncated with a [truncated] badge — inspect the JSON file directly for the rest.

Block ordering follows SCHEMA §21.3 logical groups: existence (4) → boundaries (2) → carriers (1) → internals (1) → mechanism (1) → age (1) → freq + verdict (2) → morphology + bands (2) → burden (1) → supportive (4) → synthesis (1, always last with ★ icon). Small left-margin between groups for visual sectioning.

What's NOT YET (chat-17+ backlog): per-block renderer panels (rich domain-specific rendering of e.g. internal_dynamics's recombinant table or mechanism's SD evidence) — the JSON inspector shows raw structure today; renderers will be added per-block as schema §21 fields become familiar. Stale-group warnings (chip turns amber when block's groups_used.version doesn't match current sample_groups.tsv created) — also chat-17+. SCHEMA §21.
18 structured block types [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
cluster-side Tier 2 storage · per-block axis derivation

The cluster-side evidence_registry/per_candidate/<cid>/structured/*.json emits 18 distinct block types per Quentin's t14e+ continue paste. Each block has a common header (block_type, candidate_id, region, source_script, source_version, computed_at, groups_used, keys_produced, raw_files) plus block-specific fields.

The 18 blocks at a glance: existence_layer_a/b/c/d (Q1, Q7, NONE), boundary_left/right (Q3, NONE), carrier_reconciliation (Q3/Q6/Q7, IS validation), internal_dynamics (Q2, UNCERTAIN), mechanism (Q4, NONE), age_evidence (Q5, SUPPORTED for FST sub-block), frequency (Q6, SUPPORTED), hypothesis_verdict (Q7, varies), morphology (Q1, supportive), band_composition (Q1, supportive), burden (Q6, VALIDATED), block_detect (supportive), triangle_insulation (supportive), peel_diagnostic (supportive), synteny_dollo (supportive). Plus the optional final_classification synthesis block (alias classification) that is the cluster-side's pre-computed 14-axis result.

What the scrubber does with each block: 12 of the 18 blocks (and the synthesis block) map to one or more 14-axis values via _deriveAxesFromBlock(blockType, obj). Multiple blocks compose: drop any subset of question blocks (e.g. just the 4 existence layers) → Tier view fills 4/14 axes; drop more (mechanism, age_evidence, frequency, etc.) → more axes fill; drop the final synthesis block → all 14 axes overwrite (synthesis is authoritative when present). 6 of the 18 blocks are supportive-only (morphology, band_composition, block_detect, triangle_insulation, peel_diagnostic, synteny_dollo) — they load into state.data.evidence_blocks[cid][block_type] but don't directly map to axes.

Boundary quality reconciliation: boundary_left.json + boundary_right.json are independent files; the scrubber reconciles them at end-of-load. BOTH sides need verdict ~ confirmed_structural AND hardness ≥ 0.7 to qualify as 'sharp'; otherwise 'fuzzy'. The reconciliation runs once per candidate after all files are processed.

Per-block summary on load: the alert dialog now shows known vs unknown block types loaded, count of axis derivations, count of boundary reconciliations, count of synthesis blocks that overrode per-question derivations. Not yet wired (chat-17+ backlog): per-block renderer panels on the candidate focus page, block-presence indicator chips, stale-group warnings (block's groups_used.version vs sample_groups.tsv current created), per-block JSON inspector, manuscript-bundle integration that uses block content for Methods + Results prose. SCHEMA §21.
Registry interop [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
📦 load registry button · multi-file picker

Bulk-load cluster-registry artifacts directly into the scrubber. Quentin's question: "our registry its JSON ?? So I wonder if by any luck we can just load it." Honest answer: partially. The cluster-side registry has four tables (chat-16 design); three are TSV-mirrors of JSON schemas, one is per-candidate JSON blocks. All four load cleanly as optional top-level layers in state.data.

What the 📦 button does: opens a multi-file picker. Drop any subset of these:

  • sample_registry/sample_groups.tsv → state.data.sample_groups
  • interval_registry/candidate_intervals.tsv → state.data.candidate_intervals
  • results_registry/manifest.tsv → state.data.results_manifest
  • evidence_registry/per_candidate/<cid>/structured/*.json → state.data.evidence_blocks[cid][block_type]

Special case: when a structured block of type final_classification (or classification) loads, the 14-axis values are auto-extracted into state.data.final_classification[<cid>] so the Tier sub-view (page 4) populates immediately — drop the registry's classification JSON blocks → see the 14-axis grid populated within seconds.

What does NOT load from the registry: per-window local PCA (sim_mat, |Z|, λ₁/λ₂), dosage chunks, per-window tracks. These live in the precomp JSON outside the registry. Registry-load is additive enrichment on top of a precomp JSON, not a replacement for it.

Validation: light schema-check (required columns/fields, basic types). Full JSON Schema validation is a cluster-side concern. Files that fail validation are rejected with a specific error; the rest of the load continues.

Limitations: .tsv.gz/.json.gz files need pre-extraction (browser can't transparently gunzip without `pako` library, not yet wired). Per-candidate Q matrices and FST scan tracks (results_registry/{candidate,pairwise}/...tsv.gz) are registered in the manifest but data files aren't loaded yet — chat-17+ backlog. SCHEMA §20.
Karyotype / Tier sub-view scaffold [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
14-axis classification empty state		 Page 4 renamed "5 karyotype / tier". New sub-view toggle (Karyotype | Tier ▾) inside the right pane. Karyotype subview keeps its existing per-sample regime table. New Tier subview renders a 14-axis classification grid grouped into six sections (Existence layers A-D, Boundaries, Karyotype Groups, Population Genetics, Biology, Overall Tier). When the cluster-side final_classification JSON layer (planned R-pipeline output: characterize_candidate.R + classify_inversions.R) isn't loaded, the Tier view renders an empty state showing the axis schema with categories per axis but no values. When the layer ships, axis values populate automatically — no scrubber-side change needed. Color palette: green (T1 / VALIDATED / pass / sharp), teal (T2 / SUPPORTED), amber (T3 / UNCERTAIN / fuzzy), red (T4 / SUSPECT / fail), grey (unknown). State slot karyoState.subview ∈ {karyotype, tier}, persisted to localStorage. Read-only contract — scrubber never writes to final_classification. SCHEMA §19.
Windows page coloring + ANGSD bi-SNP info [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
column-driven histogram + provenance chip		 The per-window strip on page 8 now recolors its bars by whichever sortable column the user clicks (one click sets both sort + color, so the histogram and the table tell the same story). New _winSumColorMode state. Modes: z (default), n_snps, span_kb, snp_density, lam1, lam2, ratio, mb, idx. The strip label updates dynamically (was hardcoded "|Z|"). String columns (L1, L2) fall back to z coloring since they're categorical. New ⓘ ANGSD bi-SNPs info chip below the strip — click to expand a panel listing the canonical MODULE_2A parameters used to call the biallelic SNP count for the C. gariepinus 226-sample cohort: -GL 1 -minQ 25 -minMapQ 25 -baq 1 -C 50 -setMinDepthInd 3 -setMaxDepthInd 57 -minInd 200 -SNP_pval 1e-6 -minMaf 0.05 -pest <folded SFS prior>. For other cohorts the precomp export script (planned, schema 2.15) will write these parameters as a top-level angsd_bisnp_params JSON object so the chip reads them generically rather than hard-coding per project.
Candidate bar above L1/L2 [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
pending grey · validated gold ★		 Per Quentin's request: a dedicated candidate strip now sits ABOVE the L1 and L2 zone bars in the Z panel (was previously a thin yellow ribbon below the boundary-arrow band). The new bar shows ALL candidates in state.candidateList, not just confirmed ones, with three visual states:
  • Pending (promoted but confirmed: false) — muted grey fill, no glyph. Visible from the moment of promotion so the user sees their work-in-progress without having to flip to the catalogue.
  • Confirmed (confirmed: true) — shiny gold gradient (pale shine top → mid-gold → deep gold bottom) with a warm gold outline and a ★ glyph centered in bars wider than 12 px. The "validated" state is now visually unmistakable.
  • Draft (active state.l3Draft in candidate-mode) — dashed amber outline only, no fill. Red dashed when has_warning (verdict disagrees with merge).
Render order: faint baseline → all pending (grey) → all confirmed (gold ★) → draft outline. So gold sits on top of grey if they overlap, and the draft outline sits on top of everything. New drawCandidateBar(ctx, d, toX, y0, h, opts) function with opts.dimAlpha for compact-mode dimming and opts.showStar to suppress the glyph. Backward-compat alias drawCandidateStrip forwards to the new function. Layout impact: Z panel reserves candBarH (7 px non-collapsed, 5 px collapsed) + candGap (2 px / 1 px) above the L1/L2 zone bars; plotH shrinks accordingly. 47 new tests (22 regex + 25 runtime) cover state semantics, color stops, glyph suppression on narrow bars, pass order, opts handling.
Refined catalogue export + gallery PNG [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
front-matter + JSON + manuscript-ready gallery

Catalogue export refined:

  • Front-matter metadata — TSV exports get # comment lines, Markdown gets a heading + blockquote. Includes generation timestamp, chromosome, species, schema version + layers, K distribution, σ-verdict distribution, span range, filter + sort context, display + view modes. Reviewer reading the file alone has full provenance.
  • JSON export as third format — round-trippable shape {meta, columns, rows}; meta has the same provenance as TSV/MD; columns carry key/label/type; rows are raw values (no .fmt() truncation). Useful for tools that re-load the catalogue programmatically.
  • Filenames include species slug + ISO date — e.g. C_gar_LG28_Clarias_gariepinus_inversion_catalogue_2026-04-28.tsv. Successive exports no longer overwrite.
  • Helpers: _slugForFilename(), _exportSummaryStats(), _exportFrontMatter(), _exportJsonObject().

Gallery export (📷 gallery: SVG / PNG / PDF, v4 turn 28): one panel per CONFIRMED candidate. SVG is the source of truth (vector, journal-preferred); PNG is rasterized from the SVG via canvas at 4× scale (~300+ DPI for manuscript figures); PDF opens a new window with the SVG and an A4 landscape print stylesheet, then triggers window.print() — user picks "Save as PDF" as destination. Each panel shows display name (turn 24 format, e.g. LG28.15-16Mb.A), chromosomal span (Mb), K, σ-verdict (color-coded for print: green/orange-brown/dark-red/grey/amber), and a band-count bar chart colored by groupColor(). Layout: 4 columns × auto rows. Sorted by start_bp so panels read in chromosome order, top-left to bottom-right. White background for paper printing; system + monospace fonts. Empty-state alert if no confirmed candidates.

Test coverage: 39 regex tests + 32 runtime tests (test_v399_catalogue_export_runtime.js) — verifies _slugForFilename handles edge cases, _exportSummaryStats computes correct K + verdict + span distributions, _galleryVerdictColor returns the print palette, _galleryBandCounts ignores -1 labels, exportGalleryPNG + _buildGalleryCanvas run end-to-end without throwing in a JSDOM-free sandbox.
Species portability [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
first concrete step toward §16		 The scrubber no longer hardcodes "Clarias gariepinus" anywhere user-facing. New optional species top-level JSON field; when present, surfaces in the header bar (italicized scientific name next to the chromosome) and in the manuscript prompt template ("in a {species} cohort"). Fallback when absent: header omits the field; manuscript prompt reads "in the study cohort". Existing JSONs without species continue to work unchanged. Convention: binomial scientific name. R-side export script can populate via a --species CLI flag. SCHEMA §16.6. Direct support for the toolkit-portability vision (§16.1) — one of dozens of small species-agnostic fixes that need to happen. We close the easy ones now.
Lines color-mode picker scaffold [done v3.99 t14e+ continue]
SCHEMA §15 UI scaffold for v4.0 multi-evidence renderers		 The mode-picker UI (eight options: kmeans / dosage / ghsl / het / θπ / F_ROH / family / ⚠ confounder) is now sitting in the per-sample lines header bar, sibling to the PC1/PC2 checkboxes. State slot state.linesColorMode persists to localStorage. refreshLinesColorMode() validates the chosen mode against state.layersPresent and falls back to kmeans when the source layer's missing. Greyed-out options carry tooltips naming the missing layer (e.g. "Needs dosage_chunks JSON layer"). Renderer hooks _resolveSampleColorByMode(si, gi, mode) and _resolveSampleScopeColor(si, mode) are STUBS returning null sentinel — callers fall back to existing K-means coloring. New layer per_sample_theta_pi added to detectSchemaAndLayers so the θπ option auto-enables when the future R-side STEP_R36 emit ships its layer. v4.0 work: replace stub bodies with per-mode renderers; UI is already wired so the integration is a one-step swap.
L1/L2 palette unification [done v3.99 t14e+]
ancestry + lines mini + Z zone mode + cand mini		 Four panels still rendered L1/L2 envelope bars in the pre-turn-13 washed-out palette (rgba(79,163,255) + rgba(0,230,118)): the ancestry page (page 7) zone bars, the per-sample lines panel mini L1/L2 strip, the Z panel "zone" color mode for individual |Z| dots, and the candidate-page mini-canvas L1 background tints. All updated to the turn-13 darker palette (rgba(48,116,200) + rgba(34,160,80)); ancestry page also got the "L1" / "L2" y-axis margin labels matching the Z panel. Sim_mat heatmap envelope rectangles deliberately kept in their distinct sim_mat-blue / brighter-green palette (different visual contract — drawn ON a colored heatmap, not next to a plain canvas).
Compact-mode V-bar fix [done v3.99 t14e]
applyMainGrid bails out in compact		 The Cramér's V (anchor concord) bar disappeared in compact mode and only returned after reset-layout. Cause: applyMainGrid() always set inline gridTemplateRows from the fixed-mode panelOrder, beating the compact CSS rule (40px 28px 1.5fr auto 1fr 4px var(--compact-l3fr)). The 28px anchorStripPanel row collapsed. Fix: detect compact mode, clear inline style, return early.
Empty-state with tab guide [done v3.99 t14e]
first-open isn't blank anymore		 Extended #emptyState with tool overview + per-tab one-line description (10 tabs). drawZ renders a centered "Load a precomp JSON in the sidebar (Browse…) to begin" hint when state.data is null instead of leaving the canvas silently blank.
Z panel header strip → hover tooltip [done v3.99 t14d/t14e]
⚙ + ▼ at canvas bottom-left		 The "Robust |Z| · L1/L2 zone bars + boundary peaks · click to jump" strip now lives in #zPanel title= (panel-level hover tooltip). Settings ⚙ + collapse ▼ buttons stack at canvas bottom-left (left:6px / bottom:28px and bottom:6px). #zSettingsBox popover lifted out of the now-hidden parent so it actually opens. pad.t bumped 6 → 14 so L1/L2 bars don't hit the panel top edge.
Minimap hidden when no data [done v3.99 t14d/t14e]
no more empty-square first-open		 setSimInMinimap now holds back the .active class when state.data is null (persistence still works but visual stays hidden). New _reapplyMinimapActiveOnDataLoad() reconciles after JSON load.
📊 Windows jump button [done v3.99 t14d]
left of L2 zoom in viewModeBar		 One-click jump to the windows-page table. Visually distinct from the genome / L1 zoom / L2 zoom buttons (different background) so users don't mistake it for another viewport mode.
PCA right margin fix [done v3.99 t14d]
scatter fills full panel width		 pad.r reduced 200 → 16 in both drawPCA and onPCAClick (must stay in sync for hit-detection). The 200 px gutter was reserved for an external legend; the actual legend is rendered inside the plot at top-right (lx = pad.l + plotW - legendW - 3), so the gutter was empty space.
Lock-click → immediate line recolor [done v3.99 t14d]
drawLinesPanel was missing		 🔒 lock-button click now also calls drawLinesPanel() so per-sample lines recolor instantly. Previously locking bands had no visible effect on the lines panel until the user scrubbed.
Three-axis schema 2.12 + BAM evidence 2.11 [done v3.99 t14a/t14c]
spec-only, no runtime change		 SCHEMA_V2.md 2.10 → 2.12. §12 = BAM evidence layer (9 IGV-style 1 kb tracks, weights pending calibration). §13 = three-axis framework (Tier × Completion × Characterization with 7 questions and 5 statuses). Registry width 45 → 64 cols. Convergence rules in CHARACTERIZATION_CONVERGENCE_RULES.md sibling spec.
Tooltip discoverability sweep [done v3.99 t14b]
column headers, toolbar, tabs, chips		 Every catalogue column has a tooltip field; <th> rendering wires title=. Windows page headers all titled. Top tab bar 11/11 covered. Popstats chips compose tooltip from yLabel + edgeTop/edgeBot. Ancestry chips explain maxQ_label vs delta12. Catalogue toolbar (filter, exports, select-all, count info) all titled.
Confirmed candidates in sim_mat [done v3.99 t13]
green-outlined zones + ID labels		 Confirmed candidates (provisional skipped) render as green boxes rgba(60,192,138,0.85) in the sim_mat heatmap, ID label on top.
Candidate nav strip [done v3.99 t13]
popstats / ancestry / boundaries pages		 Reusable _renderCandidateNavInline() helper. _navigateToCandidate centers cursor + refreshes pages. Whole-genome button clears state.candidate.
Z-panel L1/L2 darker bars + labels [done v3.99 t13]
deeper blue/green		 L1: rgba(48,116,200,...) (was 79,163,255). L2: rgba(34,160,80,...) (was 0,230,118). "L1"/"L2" text labels in y-axis margin.
SHIPPED · v3.99 turns 1–12
Catalogue UX overhaul [v3.99 t10–t12]
favorites, multi-select, simple/detailed		 ⭐ favorites column with persistent storage per-chrom. Ctrl/Cmd+click range select; Shift+click anchor select. simple / detailed column-set toggle. Confirmed-row visual: green left border + ✓ ID chip. Column-flash effect on header hover. Live "n SNPs → n bi-SNPs" relabel.
Z-panel ⚙ settings popover [v3.99 t9]
color/value/highlight controls hidden by default		 Wrapped color-mode + value-mode + highlight-threshold controls in a collapsible #zSettingsBox with a ⚙ toggle. localStorage persistence. Frees Z-panel canvas space; popover restructured in t14e.
Performance: L3 cache + lines pre-render [v3.99 t7–t8]
~120 ms saved per scrub step		 L3 panel cache via _renderL3Fingerprint(curL2). Lines y-matrix cache via state.__linesCache[source]. Offscreen pre-render of untracked-gray cloud blits as one drawImage (~210 polylines × 4302 segments → 1 op). Both auto-invalidate on data load.
L2 zoom mode + viewModeBar [v3.99 t8]
genome / L1 / L2 viewport scopes		 Three-state view selector: 🌍 genome (whole chromosome) / 🔎 L1 zoom (current L1 envelope) / 🔬 L2 zoom (current L2 envelope only). Persisted via localStorage. Z + tracks + lines panels all narrow to the chosen scope.
L3 resize handle 50% height [v3.99 t9]
8 → 4 px slimmer		 Compact-mode L3 resize seam shrunk so it takes less vertical space; hover/drag highlight kept visible.
Karyotype focal-chips fix [v3.99 t6]
K=6 chip rendering bug		 K=6 mode no longer collapses chips to K=3 colors. Focal-chips-above option corrected to compute per-pane palette using the active state.k.
Header / tab UX polish [v3.99 t5]
11px mono unified, no-wrap		 All header buttons standardized to 11 px monospace. viewModeBar moved into the play-row from the header. Active tab gets a vertical alpha gradient. |Z| / ±Z value-mode buttons added. Tracked-samples panel collapse arrow added.
Layout: flex-1 instead of calc(100vh – 76px) [v3.99 t4]
survives zoom changes		 Hardcoded calc didn't survive browser zoom. Now body = flex column, .wrap = flex 1, inner grid uses min-height: 0 for proper shrink. Empty viewport at the bottom gone.
Boundary refinement (page 3) [v3.97]
12 evidence tracks + auto/manual		 14 evidence tracks weighted into a combined score. Half-strongest-edges → left/right boundary zones. ±5-window default zone. Hotkeys: E/F = manual override, B = save, A = accept, R = reset. Schema §12. R-side STEP_M06_emit_boundary_evidence.R emits all source layers post-v3.98.
Manuscript bundle export [v3.18]
Markdown + TSV + JSON		 Page 4 "📝 manuscript bundle" button. Emits a Markdown file (LLM prompt + candidate tables + per-candidate detail + BibTeX + example paragraph), a TSV (raw rows), and the JSON candidate list.
SHIPPED · earlier (v3.20 → v3.98)
Schema v2 layer awareness [v3.20]
_layers_present + registry export		 Scrubber detects schema version and tracks state.layersPresent as the source of truth for conditional UI. Schema badge in header. Page 4 has "📋 export registry (TSV+JSON)" and "+ load enrichment" drag-drop. SCHEMA_V2.md documents the full contract.
Page 7 ancestry view [v3.21]
drag-drop phase4_ancestry.json		 Per-window ancestry stack (max-Q label, delta12 confidence). Drag-drop loader for <chrom>_phase4_ancestry.json. View chips for label / Q-value / delta12. Reuses the popstats stack architecture.
Page 6 popstats (v3.40s) [v3.20s]
stacked tracks aligned to chrom		 θπ, Fst, dXY, MI, Hp, Tajima's D — one-track-per-row stack rendered to a single canvas. Drag-drop additional layers. Edge labels per track explain top/bottom range.
Page 4 karyotype + page 9 confirmed [v3.16/v3.17]
per-sample band table + confirm carousel		 Page 4 per-candidate sample table: CGA, ind, family, ancestry, σ-profile, locked band, K-cluster. Sortable + filterable. TSV export. Page 9 confirmed-candidates carousel — once a candidate is marked confirmed on page 2 it joins the carousel.
Catalogue page (page 4 / now page 4) [v3.15]
